THE COURT DID NOT RECEIVE COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS PER LOCAL RULE 2.7(B). MS. PHILLIP TO PAY A $60 MOTION FILING FEE VIA E-FILING BY 4:00PM TODAY (11/28/23) IN LIEU OF FILING A NEW MOTION. THE COURT FOUND GOOD CAUSE AND GRANTED THE MOTION CONDITIONAL UPON THE PAYMENT OF THE ABOVE $60 FILING FEE BY 4:00PM TODAY. THE COURT TRIAL DATE OF 02/20/24 WILL BE CONTINUED TO 05/28/24 IN DEPT. 206 AT 9:30 AM PER STIPULATION. ALL DISCOVERY TO TRACK THE NEW TRIAL DATE. ANY FURTHER CONTINUANCE OF THE TRIAL DATE SHALL BE BY NOTICED MOTION. IF THE $60 FEE IS NOT PAID BY 4:00PM TODAY, THEN THE COURT WILL VACATE ITS RULING AND THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE OF FEB-20-2024 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206 WILL REMAIN. MS. PHILLIP TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER CONSISTENT WITH THE COURT'S RULING, APPROVED AS TO FORM. UPON THE COURT'S APPROVAL & FILING OF THE ORDER, MS. PHILLIP TO SERVE ORDER. Off Calendar Sep-20-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (Via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. SCOTT NAKAMA, Esq. of Ladva Law Firm (tel: 415-296-8844) for Plaintiff Debra Reese
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF DEBRA REESE's MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS.

The Court notes Plaintiff contests the tentative ruling. The Court inquires counsel Scott Nakama as to why he is not present in person. The Court does not allow Counsel Scott Nakama to present oral arguement, as in-person appearances are required if a party contests or does not stipulate. The Court notes it inadvertently omitted from the tentative ruling set 7 nos. 119 to 128. The Court states responses are due on October 7, 2024. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as modified.

Counsel Scott Nakama is to prepare a proposed order adding these changes and email to the Judge Pro Tem at aaron@minnisandsmallets.com .

Judge: Aaron Minnis; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (525/JPT)

Scott S. Nakama, Esq. of LADVA LAW FIRM, Tel: 415.296.8844, for plaintiff/moving party;
Corrin Phillip, Esq. of MEYERS NAVE, Tel: 619.330.1700, for defendant/opposing party.

Plaintiff's motion to compel is granted for the reasons articulated in the judge pro tem's report and recommendation, which is adopted.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim thecourt's ruling, with a copy of the judge pro tem's report and recommendation attached.
